is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study on Plasmodium falciparum infection in western Kenya

reports_target_markers.evidence: Study is about host genetics (sickle cell HbS/HbSS/HbAS genotypes) and malaria prevalence; no P. falciparum drug-resistance markers (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfdhps, Pfk13, Pfmdr1, Pfcytb) are reported

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Reports malaria prevalence by host genotype, not parasite drug-resistance codon frequencies

sub_country_location.evidence: Kisumu area, western Kenya near Lake Victoria

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Blood samples taken over a period of 12 months

african_field_samples.evidence: Field survey of West Kenyan residents

duplicate_risk.evidence: Single-site independent survey; no consortium or shared dataset mentioned

supplement.evidence: No supplementary drug-resistance data referenced; the study does not address molecular markers at all

assessment.summary: A 1997 cross-sectional survey in the Kisumu area of western Kenya examining malaria prevalence across human hemoglobin genotypes (HbAA, HbAS, HbSS). It concerns host sickle-cell genetics and malaria protection, not P. falciparum drug-resistance molecular markers. No curated resistance codons or extractable parasite genotype frequencies are reported.
exclusion_reasons: No extractable per-locus parasite resistance frequencies
